title: Meibomia callilepis
intro: Meibomia callilepis, also known as Butterfly Weed or Orange Milkweed, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Meibomia callilepis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Meibomia callilepis is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as salves and ointments, to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Meibomia callilepis is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It belongs to the genus Meibomia, which is a group of flowering plants native to North America. The species M. callilepis is further classified as a shrub or small tree, typically found in wet environments. Meibomia callilepis is also known by its synonym Lespedeza callilepis, which is sometimes used in botanical classification. The exact classification may vary depending on the source or specific taxonomic system used.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions.
